invariants_list.rule: PASS must be preceded by USER in the same session.
LIST, RETR, and STOR can only be sent after a successful 230 response from a PASS command.
A data connection establishing command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) should precede a data transfer command (LIST, RETR, STOR, NLST, APPE, MLSD).
The QUIT command may be issued at any time during the session to terminate the connection.
If PASS results in a 530 error, the client can restart the authentication sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
USER.username: Must contain the username string
PASS.password: Must contain the password string
CWD.path: Must contain a valid directory path or filename
RETR.filename: Must contain a valid directory path or filename
STOR.filename: Must contain a valid directory path or filename
PORT.address_info: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components)
RNFR.old_path: Must contain the filename to be renamed
RNTO.new_path: Must contain the new filename

recommended_actions: Several observed message types are not yet represented in the state machine; prioritize seeds and probes for: ACCT, APPE, CDUP, DELE, FEAT, HELP, MKD, MLSD
Some inferred states are weakly connected in the current model; add directed seeds for: DATA_TRANSFER

suggested_campaign.objective: Increase post-auth and data-channel coverage while validating weaker transitions.

status_changes.rationale: The USER command transitions the session from an initial state to a state awaiting password (331).
A successful PASS command (230) authenticates the user.
A failed PASS command (530) keeps the user in a non-authenticated state where they may retry.
PASV (227) sets up the passive data channel.
EPSV (229) sets up the extended passive data channel.
PORT (200) sets up the active data channel.
EPRT (200) sets up the extended active data channel.
While usually preceded by PASV/PORT, many implementations allow LIST to initiate transfer if defaults or previous settings exist; the 150 response indicates transfer start.
LIST (150) initiates a directory listing over an established data channel.
RETR (150) initiates a file retrieval over an established data channel.
STOR (150) initiates a file upload over an established data channel.
RNFR (350) transitions the state to wait for the target name.
RNTO (250) completes the rename and returns to the authenticated state.
CWD (250) changes the directory while maintaining authentication.
TYPE (200) changes the transfer type while maintaining authentication.

status_changes.description: PASS must be preceded by USER in the same session.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP authentication sequence defined in RFC 959 requires USER then PASS.

status_changes.description: If PASS results in a 530 error, the client can restart the authentication sequence with USER.
status_changes.rationale: RFC 959 allows for re-attempting authentication after failure.

status_changes.description: RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
status_changes.rationale: The rename sequence is defined as RNFR followed by RNTO.

The QUIT command may be issued at any time during the session to terminate the connection.
If PASS results in a 530 error, the client can restart the authentication sequence with USER.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
